LanguageData.VME_Royalty_Disliked.description: Titles and crowns, relics of a bygone era, draw ire more than admiration. Those who flaunt their noble status find themselves at the receiving end of equal disdain.
LanguageData.VME_Royalty_Disliked.label: disliked
LanguageData.VME_Royalty_Exalted.description: Those who drape themselves in the vestments of royalty ascend beyond mere mortals. Each step up the ladder of nobility brings greater veneration from the common folk, from a respectful nod for a Yeoman to awestruck reverence for a High Stellarch.
LanguageData.VME_Royalty_Exalted.label: exalted
LanguageData.VME_Royalty_Hated.description: Royal titles are chains that bind freedom, and each title is a shackle more cursed than the last. From Yeoman to High Stellarch, the disdain grows with the weight of the crown, heavy is the head that wears it.
LanguageData.VME_Royalty_Hated.label: hated
LanguageData.VME_Royalty_Respected.description: Every crown and every title is a symbol of burdens shouldered for the good of all. Regardless of the rank, a title earns a uniform respect, an acknowledgment of the wearer's elevated place in our order.
LanguageData.VME_Royalty_Respected.label: respected
